title: Cosmological Perturbation Theory in the Synchronous and Conformal Newtonian Gauges
notes: This paper presents a systematic treatment of the linear theory of scalar gravitational perturbations in the synchronous gauge and the conformal Newtonian (or longitudinal) gauge. It differs from others in the literature in that we give, in both gaug...

title: A Concise Introduction to Perturbation Theory in Cosmology
authors: Karim A. Malik and David R. Matravers
notes:
We give a concise, self-contained introduction to perturbation theory in cosmology at linear and
second order, striking a balance between mathematical rigour and usability. In particular we dis
cuss gauge issues and the active and passive approach to calculating gauge transformations. We
also construct gauge-invariant variables, including the second order tensor perturbation on uniform
curvature hypersurfaces.

title: Advanced Topics in Cosmology: A Pedagogical Introduction
notes: These lecture notes provide a concise, rapid and pedagogical introduction to several advanced topics in contemporary cosmology. The discussion of thermal history of the universe, linear perturbation theory, theory of CMBR temperature anisotropies and...

title: Generalizing the CPL Parametrization through Dark Sector Interaction
authors: Mikel Artola, Ruth Lazkoz, Vincenzo Salzano
notes: Investigates interacting dark energy (IDE) models with a dynamical coupling Q = 3H(δ + ηa)ρde combined with the CPL equation of state. Derives closed analytical solutions for DM and DE energy densities via confluent hypergeometric functions. Constrains 9 cosmological models using DESI DR2 BAO, Pantheon+ SNIa, and compressed Planck+ACT CMB data. Key result: constant coupling models show 2.7–2.9σ deviation from ΛCDM, but Bayesian model comparison (via Nautilus nested sampler) finds no statistical justification for IDE over ΛCDM. Emphasises Bayesian evidence over frequentist Δχ² metrics.
